You can find all the videos here:

piped.video/@FuturumEquities

One of the biggest requests I’ve gotten is for more short-form video content, so we launched a new FE YouTube series with 5–10 minute earnings breakdowns and company deep dives.

So far, we’ve covered:
• Everything you need to know about the SpaceX IPO
• Why $MELI is one of the best opportunities in the market
• Why Jensen Huang thinks $MRVL can 5x to a $1T valuation
• How $TE, $NVTS & $IREN fits into the AI power bottleneck
• Earnings reactions for $PLTR, $DOCN, $AMD, $ARM, $DDOG, $TMDX, $ASTS, $HIMS, $NBIS, $EOSE, $ONDS, $ZETA, $SNOW, $AVGO & $NVDA

I plan to keep posting 5-6 videos per week so let me know which earnings or companies you want me to cover next!

$AMD is on pace to generate over $75B in profit over the next three years: 

• 2026: $13.8B (+274% YoY)
• 2027: $24.7B (+78% YoY)
• 2028: $35.4B (+43% YoY)

AMD is the only credible challenger gaining share across both the CPU and GPU layers of the AI economy at the same time.

$NVDA CEO Jensen Huang was handing out SK Hynix x 7-Eleven HBM chips to fans in South Korea.

He joked “No HBM for you since I need all the HBM” after saying everyone loves HBM.

This is Peter Lynch’s favorite metric and I like it too because it ties valuation to growth. I prefer using a 2026 to 2028 window so the multiple adjusts for how fast the business is actually compounding.

For example $AMD may trade at ~54x forward earnings but earnings are expected to grow from $5.43 in 2026 to $16.24 in 2028 implying a ~73% earnings CAGR and a PEG of ~0.7x.

SEMICONDUCTOR STOCKS BY PEG RATIO

PEG < 1 usually means mispriced growth
 PEG > 2 starts to push into the danger zone

Fibonacci levels help you see where momentum pauses by mapping potential areas of support & resistance using ratios from the Fib sequence.  

I find them useful because strong trends often consolidate at these levels before continuing higher.

$CIFR CEO Tyler Page says the company may generate its own power on-site to move faster on AI data center demand.

Hyperscalers need power faster and Cipher may be able to “tap the pipeline” instead of waiting years on the grid.

Nvidia now has all three major memory suppliers lined up for its next-generation AI platform.

Jim Bianco: When the oil shock hits, America won't see empty gas stations - but it will still feel the pain.

Asia gets hit first (Japan is already running on strategic reserves), then Europe, then the US.

"You'll never go to a gas station and it'll have a sign that says out of gas, but you won't like the price you will see."

Oil is priced globally. A shortage anywhere raises prices everywhere.

FT @biancoresearch @BitcoinJesusETH @MilkRoadMacro.

.@biancoresearch Jim's crude oil stock drawdown analysis reframed: The Hormuz crisis is like you just lost your job. But since you're SURE you'll have no trouble getting a new job within a month, it's OK to live it up on your credit card, because your credit limit covers 6 weeks living expenses and you only need a month.

CPUs have gone from an afterthought to becoming the AI trade’s next great bottleneck - and with $AMD, $NVDA, $ARM and $INTC circling a market that is doubling nearly overnight, the only question left is which company walks away the leader. ⬇️

io-fund.com/ai-stocks/ai-cpu…

$MSFT $GOOG $IBM

$ARM $ORCL $META

Haas also told Bloomberg that he is “very confident” in reaching the company’s $15 billion AGI CPU revenue target by 2030.

Because if stocks start to crash due to slowing growth or credit event then the bond market starts to sniff out a flight to safety and lower rates due to weaker growth ahead. Besides the strong jobs number, the consumer credit situation is continuing to get weaker..   Ford just reported sales down -13.1% YoY for May. Higher rates will put pressure on autos and housing.
